content.problemStatement:
Chef and Roku are locked in an epic Rock, Paper, Scissors battle consisting of NNN games.

However, Chef has a secret weapon: he found a note revealing Roku's entire sequence of moves beforehand!

You are given a string AAA, where AiA_iAi​ represents Roku's planned move in the ithi^{th}ith game.

Each character of AAA is one of `'R'`, `'P'`, and `'S'`, denoting Rock, Paper, and Scissors, respectively.

Determine the maximum number of games Chef can win, if he is **not allowed to make the same move twice consecutively**.

**Note:** In a classic game of Rock, Paper, Scissors, both players choose one of the three moves simultaneously, and the winner is decided as follows:


Rock crushes Scissors (Rock wins)
Paper covers Rock (Paper wins)
Scissors cut Paper (Scissors wins)
If both players choose the same move, it's a draw.

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN — the number of games.
The second line of each test case contains a string AAA of length NNN, where the iii-th character AiA_iAi​ represents the move Roku will make in the iii-th game.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum number of games Chef can win.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 100000
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N, the number of games; the second line contains a string A of length N, where A_i is 'R', 'P' or 'S' representing Roku's move in the i-th game.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the maximum possible number of games Chef can win while never playing the same move in two consecutive games.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: Chef can play P S P S R, winning games 1,2,3,5. Test case 2: Chef plays R to beat S. Test case 3: Chef plays P R P R, winning games 1 and 3.

solution.approach: For each position i we keep the best score for each possible last move of Chef (R, P, S). Let dp[i][c] be the maximum wins up to game i when Chef plays move c in game i. Transition: for every previous move p (p != c) dp[i][c] = max(dp[i][c], dp[i-1][p] + win(c, A[i])). The win function returns 1 if c beats A[i] (R beats S, P beats R, S beats P) otherwise 0. Initialise dp[0][*] = 0 (no previous move). The answer for a test case is max_c dp[N-1][c]. This runs in O(N*9) = O(N) time and O(1) extra space (we keep only the previous row).
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, total O(sum of N) ≤ 1e5
